//: memattest Claude Code integration template. Replace <MEMATTEST_BIN> with the ABSOLUTE path to the venv's console script (e.g. C:/path/to/repo/.venv/Scripts/memattest or /path/to/repo/.venv/bin/memattest) — hooks run without the venv activated, so a bare 'memattest' will not resolve, and an absolute path also defeats PATH hijacking (see README Hardening). Replace <MEMORY_DIR> with your guarded memory directory as an absolute path in C:/drive notation on Windows. SessionStart runs 'hook session-start', which delivers the verify report as hook JSON (additionalContext for the agent, systemMessage for the user) and always exits 0 — Claude Code drops a SessionStart hook's stdout on non-zero exit, so plain 'memattest verify' would alert the user but never the agent. PostToolUse appends a log entry after every memory write. PreToolUse (matching shell AND file tools) denies four things: 'memattest adopt' invocations even when the executable is quoted or path-prefixed (spec 8), 'memattest install' invocations (described below), 'memattest unwatch' invocations (it narrows tamper-detection coverage, so it is gated the same way as adopt), and any agent edit — via Write/Edit or shell — of the Claude Code settings files or the disableAllHooks flag, which could otherwise un-hook memattest for the next session. All four matches are deliberately broad (fail-closed), so commands that merely mention the phrases — commit messages, greps — are denied too; rephrase and rerun. The permission deny rules below are a second, weaker layer — glob rules cannot match quoted or path-prefixed invocations. The 'memattest install' command consumes this template — filling the placeholders and merging it into a project's settings file — and PreToolUse denies agent-run 'memattest install' invocations, which would otherwise rewrite this trust surface. The last backstop is the interactive-TTY check built into 'memattest adopt' (and 'memattest unwatch') itself, which is best-effort (see README Security limitations).
